Dong Xi, styledYuandai, was fromYuyao[餘姚],Kuaiji[會稽]. He was eightchitall1and his martial strength exceededothers.A•••
Assuming onechiwas 24 centimeters at the time,Dong Xiwould have been 6.3 feet, or 192 centimeters, making him tall among his peers. “Eightchi” could also not be literal, and instead be a metaphor for how tall he was.↩
TheBook of Later Hanby Xie Cheng acclaimed[Dong] Xiof being sincere and fervent in his ambitions and integrity, and that his martial ability and resolve was fierce and powerful.
WhenSun Ceentered the commandery,[Dong] Xiwelcomed him at Gaoqian district [高遷亭].[Sun] Cemet him, and he regarded him as mighty. When they arrived [in the commandery], he was made provisionalAnti Bandit Officer under the Gates. At the time, the longstanding rebels ofShanyin[山陰],Huanglong LuoandZhou Bogathered thousands of their comrades.Sun Cemarched out to suppress them, and[Dong] Xipersonally beheaded[Huanglong] Luoand[Zhou] Bo. He then returned to be conferred asMajor of a Separate Unitand awarded thousands of troops. He was promoted toCommandant who Displays Martial Might. He followedSun Ceto attackWan[皖], suppressedLiu Xu... (truncated)
